page: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 physical cores with a single thread per core, 64.0 GB of system memory allocating 4.0 GB per core, and a bundled 300 GB local SSD. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, vector neural network instructions, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across most workloads. The server achieves average-tier results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 35388.02. Notably, single-threaded gzip compression performs in the top tier at 39.49 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 14.14 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is measured at 2685.2 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 147.28 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, text generation for small LLM models is a performance bottleneck, registering a poor score of 9.08 tokens/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a resource-dense profile suitable for cost-conscious deployments requiring significant memory and core counts without dedicated CPU guarantees. With 300 GB of local SSD storage and average performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, it is well-suited for general-purpose web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and moderate database workloads.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, a 300 GB local SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though it achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 39.49 GB/sec. Conversely, text generation for small LLM models represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom tier. This resource-dense configuration is suitable for general-purpose web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, delivering balanced general-purpose performance and top-tier compression speeds.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual server featuring sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores, sixty-four gigabytes of memory, and local solid-state storage.
bullet_points: 16 shared x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor at 2.0 GHz.
64.0 GB of system memory paired with a bundled 300 GB local SSD.
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance of 39.49 GB/sec.
Supports AVX-512, vector neural network instructions, and hardware-accelerated cryptography.
Best suited for general-purpose web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database tasks.
